text: Each key chain costs $7 1/2. Each sticker costs $2. Ella paid $50 and bought as many key chains as possible. Which expression shows how many stickers she could buy at most with the money left?
A box holds 8 cans. A supermarket has 15 full boxes and 3 extra cans. Which expression shows the total number of cans?
A ribbon is 3 m long. Mrs Chen cuts it into pieces that are each 25 cm long. Which expression shows how many pieces she can cut?
Tom had $120. He bought 4 books at $18 each and spent the rest on pens. Which expression shows how much he spent on pens?
A farmer has 240 eggs. He packs them into boxes of 12, then sells each box for $8. Which expression shows how much he earns if he sells all the boxes?
Ali saves $15 every week. After 8 weeks, he buys a toy for $62. Which expression shows how much money he has left?
A swimming pool is 50 m long. John swims 8 lengths in the morning and 5 lengths in the afternoon. Which expression shows the total distance he swims in metres?
A bag of flour weighs 2 1/2 kg. A baker uses 3/4 kg of flour each day. Which expression shows how many days the flour will last?
A rectangle has length 12 cm and width 7 cm. A square has side 5 cm. Which expression shows how much larger the area of the rectangle is than the area of the square?
A school ordered 25 boxes of coloured pencils with 24 pencils in each box. The pencils are shared equally among 6 classes. Which expression shows how many pencils each class gets?
Mrs Lam baked 8 trays of cookies with 12 cookies on each tray. She gave 24 cookies to her neighbours and packed the rest equally into 6 bags. Which expression shows how many cookies are in each bag?
A bus travels at 60 km/h. A train travels 3 times as fast as the bus. Which expression shows how far the train travels in 2 1/2 hours?
A cinema has 18 rows with 24 seats in each row. 315 seats are occupied. Which expression shows how many seats are empty?
David earns $24 per hour. He worked for 8 hours on Saturday and 6 hours on Sunday. Which expression shows his total earnings for the weekend?
A shop had 480 apples. It sold 1/3 of them in the morning. In the afternoon it sold 1/4 of the remaining apples. Which expression shows how many apples were sold in the afternoon?
A rectangular tank is 40 cm long, 25 cm wide and 30 cm tall. Water fills 2/3 of the tank. Which expression shows the volume of water in the tank in cm³?
Peter has 3 times as many marbles as John. Together they have 84 marbles. Which expression shows how many marbles Peter has?
A rope that is 7 1/2 m long is cut into pieces each 1/2 m long. Which expression shows how many pieces there are?
Museum tickets cost $12 for adults and $7 for children. A family of 2 adults and 3 children visits the museum. Which expression shows the total ticket cost?
Water flows into a tank at 8 litres per minute. After 15 minutes, 40 litres of water are removed. Which expression shows how many litres remain in the tank?
Sarah is x years old. Her mother is 4 times her age. In 5 years' time, which expression shows her mother's age?
A stack of 5 identical books is 15 cm tall. Which expression shows the height of a stack of 8 such books?
3 out of every 5 students in a school are girls. The school has 450 students altogether. Which expression shows how many boys there are?
A number is multiplied by 6, then 18 is added to the result. The final answer is 54. Which expression shows how to find the original number?
Three friends have $200 altogether. They buy a gift for $95 and share the remaining money equally. Which expression shows each person's share?
